openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6.output: {"A":"Answer A provides a single complete HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. It correctly implements the joke generator with 4 jokes, a button, and random selection. It goes well beyond the basic requirements with confetti effects, 3D card tilt, animations, a joke counter, and Tailwind CSS styling. The code is functional and self-contained. However, it includes significant extra complexity (confetti canvas, blob animations, icon toggling) that wasn't requested.","B":"Answer B provides a clean, well-structured solution split across three files (HTML, CSS, JS) with thorough comments explaining each part. It correctly implements all requirements: 4 jokes, a button, and random display. The code is clear, beginner-friendly, and includes helpful explanations. It also provides usage instructions. The styling is clean and functional without unnecessary complexity.","winner":"B","reason":"Both answers correctly implement the required functionality, but Answer B is superior in clarity, conciseness, and educational value. It separates concerns properly (HTML/CSS/JS), includes excellent comments explaining the logic, and stays focused on what was asked without excessive over-engineering. Answer A, while impressive technically, adds many unrequested features (confetti, 3D tilt, animations, counters) that make it harder to understand and more complex than necessary for a 'simple website'. Answer B is the better response for someone who just wants a simple joke website."}

messages.content: Create a simple website using HTML. When the user clicks a button, one of four jokes should be displayed at random.
